comment:
## Done — Pipeline onboarding complete

Ran the full 3D generation pipeline end-to-end on a medieval wooden barrel:

### Pipeline run results

| Step | Tool | Time | Cost | Output |
|------|------|------|------|--------|
| Concept image | Grok `grok-imagine-image` | ~3s | $0.02 | Barrel on white BG |
| Prototype | Hunyuan3D v2 (image-to-3D) | ~12s | $0.16 | 518K faces, 11.9MB, untextured |
| Production | Hunyuan3D v3 (image-to-3D) | ~133s | $0.375 | 500K faces, 34.5MB, textured |
| Decimate (prototype) | Blender decimate.py | ~20s | Free | 3,965 faces, 0.16MB |
| Decimate (production) | Blender decimate.py | ~18s | Free | 4,949 faces, 1.32MB |

**Total cost:** ~$0.555 for the full pipeline including prototype validation.

### Confirmed findings from CEO's testing

- **Image-to-3D >> text-to-3D.** The barrel produced clean, accurate geometry matching the concept image closely.
- **Grok prompt formula works.** "white background, front-facing view, centered, single object, product photography style" consistently produces clean references.
- **Prototype tier is essential for validation.** 12 seconds and $0.16 to confirm the shape before committing to the $0.375 production run.
- **Decimate script is reliable.** 500K → ~5K faces with baked textures in under 30 seconds. Production model went from 34.5MB → 1.32MB.
- **Production tier cold start is real.** ~2 minutes total including queue time. Submit early and do other work while waiting.
- **Write JSON to files, never inline in PowerShell.** Confirmed — PowerShell quoting corrupts JSON.

### Settings used
- Decimate: `voxel_size=0.004`, `target_faces=2500` (medium detail, barrel)
- Result was 4,949 faces — the voxel remesh step determines the lower bound more than the target

### Ready for production

I'm fully onboarded on the pipeline and ready to generate game-ready 3D assets. Files saved in `memories/3d-experiments/`.

Thumbnail: ![barrel](https://v3b.fal.media/files/b/0a91cdd9/JsShs8pSZ40tDT8jarqTK_preview.png)
